key_insight: A polygonal cell-based smoothed finite element framework can robustly handle nonlinear geotechnical problems with complex meshes, staged loading, and local failure while reducing sensitivity to mesh distortion.
engineering_relevance: The method is directly useful for practical geotechnical design and analysis because it improves prediction of displacement, stress, plastic strain, bearing capacity, and slope safety in realistic soil-structure systems.
method: Cell-based strain smoothing with boundary integration over smoothing subcells
Incremental elasto-plastic updates with Mohr-Coulomb and Duncan-Chang models
evidence_signal: The abstract reports verification on benchmark and engineering cases including a perforated plate, strip footing, dam, tunnel excavation, and slope stability, with accurate response predictions.
watch_item: The approach requires a user element implementation in ABAQUS and careful handling of nonlinear constitutive updates and polygonal/hanging-node mesh generation.
paper_title: Nonlinear Geotechnical Analysis Using a Polygonal Cell-Based Smoothed Finite Element Framework
